Photo from AIMember-only story

A practical breakdown of the two disciplines reshaping how production AI agents get built in 2026, plus a framework for figuring out which one your project is missing.

8 min read
Just now

--

An AI agent that spins in circles forever and an AI agent that never starts without you typing something have the same exact root cause.
Nobody figured out which of two very different problems they were actually solving.
A team I know spent three weeks fighting the first kind. Their coding agent would make an edit, break a test, fix that test, break a different one, and loop like that forever without ever landing.
Their fix was to bolt on another retry layer. It made things worse.
The problem was never the loop. Nothing was checking whether an edit was safe before the agent tried it in the first place.
They needed a harness, not another loop, and it took them three weeks to see the difference.
That exact mixup is happening across a lot of teams right now, and it’s costing weeks, sometimes months.
